rationale: Fix in store.go correctly threads workingDir through detectPrefix and resolves the worktree-cwd contamination. However, the new test does not simulate the bug scenario: it never chdirs into a worktree-shaped path, so it would pass on main too — failing AC #2's 'fails-before-fix, passes-after' requirement. Layer-2 bead and deferral note are acceptable.

findings.summary: TestGenID_WorktreePrefix never sets cwd to a '.execute-bead-wt-...' path. With cwd inside the ddx repo (the default for `go test`), pre-fix detectPrefix() would return filepath.Base of the ddx git toplevel ('ddx'), so the test passes both before and after the fix. AC #2 explicitly requires the test to fail on current main.
TestDetectPrefix_WorktreeDir only exercises the new signature, not the regression: it doesn't chdir to a worktree dir to prove cwd no longer contaminates the prefix.
Fix itself looks correct: workingDir hoisted out of config-load block and passed to detectPrefix; gitpkg.Command now uses the project root.
AC #3 deferral path taken — 22 (not 12) malformed beads remain, deferred to mass-repair pending layer-2 decision; recorded in bead notes.
AC #4 layer-2 design bead ddx-d3a4f89c referenced in notes.
